change_log.reason: Phase-1 evaluator remediation after scope freeze
change_log.diff_summary: Expanded search target coverage, apply concurrency and recovery semantics, rollback tracked/untracked handling, environment leak hardening, and synchronized sidecar/execution guard.

change_log.reason: PM runner compatibility remediation after scope freeze
change_log.diff_summary: Added phase_id and task_id aliases alongside canonical id fields so snoworca-pm v3 state initialization and child prompts can consume the sidecar deterministically.

phases.title: Package skeleton and contract freeze
phases.goal: Turn the current npm metadata into an executable TypeScript ESM package and freeze Core DTO contracts before feature work.

tasks.title: Complete package skeleton, scripts, and CLI binary
method_signatures: export async function main(argv: string[], env?: NodeJS.ProcessEnv): Promise<number>
export function buildProgram(): Command
tasks.reference_pattern: package.json is already initialized for name/type/engine; use it as the edit base, not as final package contract.
implementation_guide: Install TypeScript, Vitest, ESLint, commander, yaml, ajv, minisearch, and MCP SDK dependencies.
Add bin mapping so global install exposes speckiwi.
Create ESM TypeScript config with declaration output.
Implement --help smoke path through bin/speckiwi.
tasks.rationale: The SRS requires Node >=20, ESM TypeScript, global CLI install, and no native runtime dependency before any Core behavior ships.
pitfalls: Do not introduce a DB package or HTTP server package.
Keep bin script stdout limited to CLI output paths.
tasks.test_guidance: Create test/smoke/package.test.ts with success, missing command, and help-output boundary cases.
dod: npm run build exits 0
npm test -- package exits 0
node bin/speckiwi --help exits 0
package name remains speckiwi

tasks.title: Define Core DTO, input DTO, diagnostics, and JSON renderer contracts
method_signatures: export type CoreResult<T> = { ok: true; data: T; diagnostics: DiagnosticBag } | ErrorResult
export function renderJson(result: unknown): string
export function toStructuredContent<T>(result: CoreResult<T>): Record<string, unknown>
tasks.reference_pattern: Implementation readiness lines 45-80 define the DiagnosticBag and DTO sharing rule.
implementation_guide: Create DTO types before Core service code.
Keep diagnostics as bag fields errors/warnings/info.
Use the same DTO object for CLI JSON and MCP structuredContent.
Add fixtures that assert deterministic JSON shape.
tasks.rationale: Freezing DTOs prevents CLI and MCP from diverging while later modules are added.
pitfalls: Do not return arrays as diagnostics shorthand.
Do not let CLI human formatting leak into Core DTOs.
tasks.test_guidance: Create contract tests for success, error, validation-result, and JSON rendering order.
dod: CoreResult and ValidateResult fixtures pass
CLI JSON renderer outputs one JSON object
MCP adapter helper reuses Core DTO

rollback.command: 1) Restore tracked edits for the task-owned paths with git restore -- <tracked owns>. 2) Delete only untracked task-owned files and directories listed in owns, then run the task verification command to confirm rollback.

phases.title: Workspace init, file store, and YAML load
phases.goal: Create and load repository-local .speckiwi workspaces without crossing root boundaries.

tasks.title: Implement workspace root discovery, root override, and safe file store
method_signatures: export function findWorkspaceRoot(start: string, explicitRoot?: string): Promise<WorkspaceRoot>
export function resolveStorePath(root: WorkspaceRoot, storePath: StorePath): WorkspacePath
export async function atomicWriteText(path: string, text: string): Promise<void>
tasks.reference_pattern: 없음 - 신규 패턴. Path policy comes from SRS storage and security requirements.
implementation_guide: Walk upward from cwd until .speckiwi is found.
Let --root override discovery after resolving to an absolute path.
Normalize StorePath to POSIX separators.
Reject path traversal and writes outside .speckiwi except export targets.
tasks.rationale: Every later command depends on a trustworthy root and file boundary.
pitfalls: Windows drive paths must not be treated as StorePath.
Cache and exports are generated outputs, not source truth.
tasks.test_guidance: Add tests for discovered root, explicit root, traversal rejection, and atomic write failure cleanup.
dod: Traversal tests reject ../ paths
Explicit root wins over discovery
Atomic write leaves no partial target on injected failure

tasks.title: Implement speckiwi init templates and YAML parser subset
method_signatures: export async function initWorkspace(input: InitInput): Promise<InitResult>
export async function loadYamlDocument(path: WorkspacePath): Promise<LoadedYamlDocument>
tasks.reference_pattern: The init tree and YAML subset policy are specified in SRS and readiness docs.
implementation_guide: Generate index.yaml, overview.yaml, dictionary.yaml and required subdirectories.
Use yaml parseDocument to collect errors and source locations.
Detect anchors, aliases, and merge keys as diagnostics.
Return diagnostics without process crashes.
tasks.rationale: Init and YAML load establish the source-of-truth model for all commands.
pitfalls: Do not serialize comments as a v1 requirement.
Do not register index.yaml as a content document.
tasks.test_guidance: Test success init, existing workspace rejection, invalid YAML parse, anchor/alias/merge rejection.
dod: speckiwi init creates the full directory tree
Invalid YAML returns line/column diagnostics
Anchor and alias fixtures fail validation

phases.title: Schema validation and diagnostics
phases.goal: Validate all supported YAML document types with deterministic diagnostics.

tasks.title: Add JSON Schema files and Ajv2020 strict compile layer
method_signatures: export function compileSchemas(): SchemaRegistry
export function validateAgainstSchema(kind: SchemaKind, value: unknown): DiagnosticBag
tasks.reference_pattern: Schema dialect and Ajv2020 strict mode are contract decisions.
implementation_guide: Create one schema per document type.
Use additionalProperties false except documented metadata extension points.
Compile all schemas at startup in tests.
Return schema diagnostics through DiagnosticBag.
tasks.rationale: Schema validation must fail deterministically before graph, search, or apply logic reads data.
pitfalls: Do not allow proposal documents in index.documents.
Keep status enums document-type specific.
tasks.test_guidance: Add valid and invalid fixture for each schema plus strict compile test.
dod: All schemas compile under Ajv2020 strict mode
Invalid fixtures emit stable code/path diagnostics
Schema files are included in package files

tasks.title: Implement semantic validation registry and validate command service
method_signatures: export async function validateWorkspace(input: ValidateInput): Promise<ValidateResult>
export function validateRegistry(workspace: LoadedWorkspace): DiagnosticBag
tasks.reference_pattern: Validation code mapping is governed by validation spec and readiness decisions.
implementation_guide: Build document, scope, requirement, and relation registries from schema-valid documents.
Suppress cascading reference errors from schema-invalid documents.
Detect duplicate IDs, missing files, unregistered content, relation cycles, and warnings.
Expose ValidateResult for CLI and MCP.
tasks.rationale: Semantic validation protects proposal/apply and keeps diagnostics actionable.
pitfalls: Do not validate references from documents that failed parse or schema validation.
Do not treat warnings as apply blockers unless errors exist.
tasks.test_guidance: Add fixtures for each VAL-ERR and VAL-WARN family with stable diagnostic snapshots.
dod: All listed validation error families have at least one fixture
ValidateResult keeps deterministic ordering
Warnings do not set process failure in Core

phases.title: Graph, requirement lookup, trace, impact, and ID generation
phases.goal: Build deterministic graph structures and requirement access primitives.

tasks.title: Build registries, graph builder, trace, and impact services
method_signatures: export function buildGraph(workspace: ValidWorkspace): GraphResult
export function traceRequirement(input: TraceRequirementInput, graph: GraphResult): TraceResult
export function impactRequirement(input: ImpactInput, graph: GraphResult): ImpactResult
tasks.reference_pattern: Graph nodes and deterministic ordering are fixed by readiness decisions.
implementation_guide: Create deterministic node IDs for document, scope, and requirement nodes.
Sort nodes and edges lexicographically by stable keys.
Implement incoming and outgoing requirement relations.
Add depth-limited upstream, downstream, and both-direction trace.
tasks.rationale: Search, impact, CLI, and MCP all rely on consistent graph output.
pitfalls: Do not include index.yaml as a graph document node.
Do not mutate workspace during trace or impact.
tasks.test_guidance: Test document graph, requirement graph, cycle behavior, stable ordering, trace depth, and impact context toggles.
dod: GraphResult ordering is snapshot-stable
TraceResult includes root requirement
ImpactResult lists downstream affected requirements

tasks.title: Implement requirement ID preview and generation policy

export function assertExplicitRequirementId(id: string, registry: RequirementRegistry): DiagnosticBag
tasks.reference_pattern: Requirement ID generation is made concrete in readiness decisions.
implementation_guide: Map requirement type to prefix.
Derive scope segment from scope id.
Find max existing sequence in the same prefix/project/scope tuple.
Reject duplicate explicit IDs.
Keep preview read-only.
tasks.rationale: ID creation must be deterministic to keep proposal and apply flows reproducible.
pitfalls: Preview must not create proposal, YAML, or cache files.
Document ID and requirement ID namespaces stay separate.
tasks.test_guidance: Test next sequence, collision retry, explicit ID priority, duplicate explicit ID, and read-only preview.
dod: Generated IDs match the documented prefix and sequence policy
Duplicate explicit IDs return ErrorResult

phases.title: Search and cache
phases.goal: Provide exact/BM25/Korean search and regenerated JSON caches.

tasks.title: Implement flat document indexing, exact lookup, BM25, Korean n-gram, and dictionary expansion
method_signatures: export function flattenWorkspace(workspace: ValidWorkspace): SearchDocument[]
export function tokenizeKorean(input: string): string[]
export function search(input: SearchInput, index: SearchIndex): SearchResultSet
tasks.reference_pattern: Search spec defines exact index, BM25, Korean n-gram, and dictionary expansion.
implementation_guide: Flatten every SRS search target into typed SearchDocument rows: overview goals, overview nonGoals, overview glossary, PRD items, SRS requirements, technical sections, ADR records, rule documents, document metadata, and scope metadata.
Index exact IDs for requirement, document, scope, PRD item, technical section, ADR, and rule entities.

Index title, statement, rationale, description, tags, acceptanceCriteria, glossary terms, section headings, ADR decisions, rule text, and metadata keywords into BM25 fields with explicit field boosts.
Use Korean 2-gram and 3-gram tokenizer for Korean text.
Apply dictionary synonym expansion before BM25 ranking.
Return entity type, source path, score, matchedFields, and stable ordering for ties.
tasks.rationale: Local search is the main user and agent discovery path, so exact and semantic-ish modes must share source data.
pitfalls: Do not add vector DB dependency.
Exact ID lookup must not be hidden by BM25 scoring.
tasks.test_guidance: Test exact ID for requirement/document/scope/PRD item/technical section/ADR/rule, overview goals/nonGoals/glossary indexing, requirement fields, filters, pagination, score ordering, matchedFields, Korean phrase search, and synonym expansion.
dod: Search result items contain entity type, score, source path, and matchedFields
Exact ID tests cover requirement, document, scope, PRD item, technical section, ADR, and rule entities
BM25 tests cover overview goals, overview nonGoals, overview glossary, requirement fields, PRD item text, technical section text, ADR decision text, and rule text
Korean tokenizer tests pass
Dictionary absence still permits base search

tasks.title: Implement cache manifest, stale detection, rebuild, clean, and degraded fallback
method_signatures: export async function rebuildCache(input: CacheRebuildInput): Promise<CacheResult>
export async function cleanCache(input: CacheCleanInput): Promise<CacheResult>
export function isCacheStale(manifest: CacheManifest, inputs: CacheInputs): boolean
tasks.reference_pattern: Readiness decisions define deterministic cache manifest path and hash behavior.
implementation_guide: Hash input YAML and package/schema versions.
Write graph/search/diagnostics caches only after successful rebuild.
Remove partial outputs on rebuild failure.
Let --no-cache bypass read and write.
Keep cache clean inside .speckiwi/cache.
tasks.rationale: Cache must improve performance without becoming source truth.
pitfalls: Do not store timestamps, host paths, or OS separators in cache manifest.
Cache corruption must degrade to YAML load.
tasks.test_guidance: Test stale detection, corruption fallback, rebuild output, clean output, and no-cache bypass.
dod: cache rebuild creates manifest and section caches
cache clean preserves non-cache files
corrupt cache falls back to YAML path

export function mapCoreResultToExitCode(result: CoreResult<unknown>): number
export async function doctor(input: DoctorInput): Promise<DoctorResult>
tasks.reference_pattern: CLI JSON stdout and exit codes are fixed by CLI spec and readiness decisions.
implementation_guide: Parse --root, --json, --no-cache, and pagination options consistently.
Route human output through human renderer.
Route JSON output through Core DTO renderer only.
Map validation and apply failures to documented exit codes.
Doctor checks Node version, workspace files, YAML parse, cache state, MCP binary path, and stdout policy risk.
tasks.rationale: CLI behavior is the primary developer surface and must remain parseable for agents.
pitfalls: Do not write diagnostic logs to stdout in --json mode.
Do not let command handlers throw uncaught errors.
tasks.test_guidance: Use child process tests for stdout, stderr, and exit code across success and failure paths.

doctor supports human and JSON output

Preserve common --root and --json behavior.
Support list filters and search filters.
Normalize graph plural aliases to canonical singular values.
Render IDs, paths, scores, and diagnostics in human output.

pitfalls: Do not reimplement Core filtering in CLI.
Impact v1 is requirement-ID only.

phases.title: Proposal, apply, and requirement mutations
phases.goal: Support safe agent writes through proposal-first behavior and validated apply.

tasks.title: Implement proposal schema, hashes, stale detection, and propose mode
method_signatures: export function createProposal(input: ProposeChangeInput, workspace: ValidWorkspace): Promise<ProposalResult>
export function canonicalJsonHash(value: unknown): string
export function buildPatchOperations(change: ProposedChange): ProposalOperation[]
tasks.reference_pattern: Proposal target shape, targetHash, and stale behavior are fixed by readiness decisions.
implementation_guide: Store proposals under .speckiwi/proposals.
Use discriminated target.kind instead of legacy target.path.
Compute base target hash with RFC 8785 canonical JSON.
Implement req create and update as proposal-producing operations by default.
Never change source YAML in propose mode.
tasks.rationale: Proposal-first behavior reduces accidental source mutation by agents.
pitfalls: Root JSON Pointer document replacement is forbidden.
Preview ID is read-only and not part of proposal stale flow.
tasks.test_guidance: Test proposal file shape, no source mutation, hash determinism, stale proposal detection, duplicate explicit ID rejection.
dod: Propose mode writes only proposal YAML
Stored proposal schema rejects target.path
Target hash snapshots are stable

rollback.command: 1) Restore tracked edits for the task-owned paths with git restore -- <tracked owns>. 2) Delete only untracked task-owned files and directories listed in owns, then run the task verification command to confirm rollback.

tasks.title: Implement apply engine, atomic writes, cache stale marking, and req create/update CLI
method_signatures: export async function applyChange(input: ApplyChangeInput): Promise<ApplyResult>
export function applyProposalToDocument(document: LoadedYamlDocument, proposal: ProposalDocument): UpdatedYamlDocument
export async function withTargetWriteLock<T>(target: StorePath, fn: () => Promise<T>): Promise<T>
export function registerRequirementWriteCommands(program: Command): void
tasks.reference_pattern: Apply input union and confirm=true rule are defined in readiness decisions.
implementation_guide: Require exactly one of proposalId, proposalPath, or inline change.
Require confirm true for apply.
Run validation before writing.
Acquire a per-target write lock before reading the final target for mutation.
Recheck the current base hash immediately before rename and return a deterministic conflict error if it changed after validation.
Use unique temp paths per process and target.
Reject stale proposal hash mismatches.
Create a rollback-capable backup or temp copy for the target document before rename and include the backup path in ApplyResult diagnostics when a write fails.
Write via temporary file then atomic rename.
Mark related caches stale after successful write only after the target rename succeeds.
Return failure cause and recovery steps for stale, validation, permission, lock, and atomic write failures.
tasks.rationale: Apply is the highest-risk feature and must be narrow, validated, and recoverable.
pitfalls: Do not accept apply when allowApply=false.
Do not apply if validation errors remain.
Do not leave partial writes after failure.
tasks.test_guidance: Test confirm missing, stale proposal, validation error, permission failure, lock conflict, concurrent apply attempts against the same YAML file, stale-after-validation race, atomic write failure, backup/temp copy reporting, recovery guidance in ErrorResult, successful apply, and cache stale marker consistency.
dod: Apply rejects stale proposals
Concurrent apply attempts to the same target yield one success and deterministic conflict or stale error for the loser
Base hash is rechecked after validation and before rename
Atomic write failure leaves original YAML unchanged and reports recovery steps
Backup or rollback-capable temp copy behavior is covered by tests
Cache stale marker is written only after successful target rename
req update without --apply creates proposal

phases.title: MCP stdio adapter
phases.goal: Expose Core services to AI coding agents through stdio MCP tools and resources.

tasks.title: Implement MCP server, stdio transport, tools, and input schemas
method_signatures: export async function runMcpServer(input: McpServerInput): Promise<void>
export function registerMcpTools(server: McpServer, core: SpecKiwiCore): void
export function toolResultFromCore<T>(result: CoreResult<T>): CallToolResult
tasks.reference_pattern: MCP tools and structuredContent rules are listed in MCP tool spec.
implementation_guide: Start stdio server only from speckiwi mcp.
Bind root from CLI argument and reject tool root override.
Register all required tools with JSON-compatible input schemas.
Return Core DTO through structuredContent and JSON text content.
Write logs to stderr only.
tasks.rationale: MCP is the AI-agent interface and must mirror Core contracts without opening network ports.
pitfalls: Do not emit diagnostic logs to stdout.
Do not create HTTP JSON-RPC endpoints.
Apply tool must respect allowApply and validation gates.
tasks.test_guidance: Use in-process MCP tool tests for tool schemas, read-only calls, apply rejection, and stdout policy.
dod: All 13 MCP tools are registered
MCP stdout contains protocol output only
Tool root override is rejected

export async function readMcpResource(uri: string, core: SpecKiwiCore): Promise<ReadResourceResult>
tasks.reference_pattern: MCP resources use ReadResourceResult.contents and not Core DTO envelopes.
implementation_guide: Parse speckiwi://overview and speckiwi://index.
Serve registered document YAML by document ID.
Serve stable JSON requirement and scope contexts.
Reject malformed and unknown URIs with documented JSON-RPC errors.
tasks.rationale: Resources provide low-friction context loading for agents without extra tool calls.
pitfalls: Do not wrap resources in CoreResult envelope.
Do not read paths outside the workspace root.
tasks.test_guidance: Test each resource URI, malformed URI, unknown ID, MIME type, and root boundary.

method_signatures: export async function exportMarkdown(input: ExportMarkdownInput): Promise<ExportResult>
export function renderDocumentMarkdown(document: ContentDocument): string

implementation_guide: Run validation before export when requested.
Render only exportable document types.
Create index.md from content document registry, not index.yaml as a source document.
Write paths relative to outputRoot in ExportResult.
Keep Markdown output reproducible from YAML.

pitfalls: Do not support Markdown import in v1.
Do not export rule and dictionary types unless a future spec adds support.

phases.title: Hardening, performance, and release readiness
phases.goal: Close security, reliability, cross-platform, performance, and packaging release gates.

tasks.title: Add hardening suites for security, reliability, cross-platform, and performance targets
method_signatures: export function buildLargeWorkspaceFixture(options: LargeWorkspaceOptions): Promise<FixtureWorkspace>
export function assertNoDbOrHttpArtifacts(root: string): Promise<void>
tasks.reference_pattern: Release and hardening checklist are specified in SRS acceptance criteria and implementation plan.
implementation_guide: Add traversal, invalid YAML, duplicate ID, cache corruption, MCP stdout, Windows path, and large workspace tests.
Check no SQLite or DB artifacts are created.
Check no HTTP listener is opened during commands.

Inject sentinel environment variable values and assert they are not serialized into .speckiwi/**/*.yaml, proposals, cache files, diagnostics, CLI JSON, MCP structuredContent, or Markdown export unless passed as explicit user content.
Measure performance targets as threshold tests with local skip metadata for unstable CI.
tasks.rationale: v1 release is defined by negative guarantees as much as feature success.
pitfalls: Performance tests must be deterministic enough for local runs.
Hardening tests should use temp directories, not developer home paths.
tasks.test_guidance: Run security, reliability, cross-platform, environment-leak, and perf suites separately and through npm test.
dod: Path traversal is rejected
No DB or HTTP artifacts are observed
Sentinel environment variable values are absent from generated YAML, proposals, cache, diagnostics, CLI JSON, MCP structuredContent, and export output unless explicitly supplied as user content
Cache corruption degrades to YAML load
Performance target tests report measured timings

Run MCP smoke startup with a temp root.

risks.risk: Validation and proposal/apply semantics have many cross-document invariants.
risks.mitigation: Implement semantic validation before write features and keep apply tests strict.

risks.risk: MCP stdout logging can break protocol transport.
risks.mitigation: Add process-level stdout/stderr tests before considering MCP complete.

risks.risk: Search performance target depends on fixture size and local IO.
risks.mitigation: Separate correctness tests from measured performance reports and keep cache fallback deterministic.
